queries.query: Show me the full text content stored inside a file on disk
Open several documents at once and return all of their contents together
Save this text as a brand-new file, replacing whatever was there before
Change a handful of specific lines inside an existing text document
Give me a listing of everything contained in a particular folder
List a folder's contents and tell me how large each item is
Produce a nested map of a directory and all of its subfolders
Locate files anywhere beneath a folder whose names match a wildcard pattern
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of memory:search_nodes (both 'search', but this is a filename lookup on disk, not a graph query)

queries.query: Make sure a nested folder path exists, creating any missing parents
Rename a file or relocate it into a different directory
Get detailed metadata such as size and timestamps for one file
Load an image and return its base64 data along with the mime type
Which directories is this sandbox actually permitted to touch?
Put my modified files into the staging area for the next commit
Permanently record my staged changes into the project's revision history
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of memory:add_observations (both 'record/add', but this writes a VCS commit, not graph facts)

queries.query: Tell me which files are currently modified, staged, or untracked
Show the history of past commits in this repository
What edits have I made in my working copy that aren't staged yet?
Show only the changes I've already added to the index
Compare two branches and show how they differ
List all of the branches that exist in this repository
Start a new branch based off the current one
Switch my working copy over to a different branch
Display the full contents and changes of one specific commit
Unstage everything I've added, putting it back into the working directory
Add brand-new nodes to my knowledge graph
Connect two existing nodes together with a labeled relationship
Attach a few additional facts to a node I already stored
Find stored notes in my knowledge base that mention a particular topic
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of filesystem:search_files (both 'search', but this queries the graph, not the filesystem)

queries.query: Dump out my entire knowledge graph at once
Retrieve a couple of specific nodes by their exact names
Remove some nodes and every link attached to them
Delete particular relationships between stored nodes
Strip specific facts off a node without deleting the node itself
Run a query to pull matching rows out of my local relational database
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of filesystem:read_text_file (both 'read', but this is a SQL SELECT, not reading a file)

queries.query: Insert, update, or delete records in the database
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of filesystem:write_file (both 'write', but this mutates DB rows, not a file)

queries.query: Define a new table with its columns in the database
Show me every table that exists in this database
What columns and types does a given table have?
Record a business takeaway into the running analysis memo
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of memory:add_observations (both append a fact, but this targets the SQLite insight memo)

queries.query: Download a web page from a URL and give me its content as markdown
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of filesystem:read_text_file (both return text, but this retrieves a remote URL, not a local file)

queries.query: What is the current wall-clock time in a given city's timezone?
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of time:convert_time (same server, but this reports 'now', it does not translate a supplied timestamp)

queries.query: Translate a specific timestamp from one timezone into another
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of time:get_current_time (same server, but this converts a given time rather than reading the clock)

queries.query: Help me reason through a hard problem step by step, revising my thoughts as I go
I want to persist a new piece of structured information so I can recall it later
queries.notes: graded: 'persist information' is genuinely ambiguous across graph/DB/file backends

queries.query: Look back over the recorded history of what changed and when
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of memory:read_graph (both surface stored state, but 'history of changes' means the VCS log)

queries.query: Get rid of some stored records I no longer need
queries.notes: hard-negative: near-dup of filesystem:move_file (deletion is not relocation); graded across DB/graph
